problem_addressed: Scientific discovery is a lengthy and costly process that demands substantial time and resources from initial conception to final results.
core_claim: Agent Laboratory, an autonomous LLM-based framework, can complete the entire research process from literature review to report writing, producing comprehensive research outputs while enabling human feedback at each stage.
key_contributions: Introduction of Agent Laboratory, an autonomous LLM-based framework for completing the entire research process
Demonstration that Agent Laboratory can significantly reduce research expenses (84% decrease compared to previous autonomous research methods)
Finding that Agent Laboratory driven by o1-preview generates the best research outcomes
Finding that the generated machine learning code achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing methods
novelty: Agent Laboratory is presented as a framework capable of completing the entire research process autonomously, from literature review to experimentation to report writing, producing comprehensive research outputs including code repositories and research reports.
method_summary: The method involves an autonomous LLM-based framework that accepts a human-provided research idea and progresses through three stages: literature review, experimentation, and report writing, producing comprehensive research outputs including a code repository and a research report, while enabling users to provide feedback and guidance at each stage.
method_components: Human feedback mechanism at each stage
key_innovation: The autonomous LLM-based framework that can complete the entire research process from start to finish, with the ability to produce comprehensive research outputs and enable human feedback at each stage.
assumptions: That LLM agents can effectively perform literature review, experimentation, and report writing
That human feedback at each stage can improve the overall quality of research
That the framework can produce comprehensive research outputs including code repositories and research reports
title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
